title: Welcome to Kamigotchi World
introDialogue:
MENU: “Hi there, Player! Welcome to Kamigotchi World!”
MENU: “You can call me Menu. I’ll be your guide!”
MENU: “I’ll also track your items, quests, and companions while you explore.”
MENU: “You can navigate by clicking on the map to pilot your Operator.”
MENU: “Start by exploring my options and clicking around the screen.”
MENU: “When you’ve seen the basics, we can move on.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “See - that wasn’t too tricky, was it?”
MENU: “Good job!”

title: Beginning Your Journey
introDialogue:
MENU: “Feel like you’re getting the hang of my interface?”
MENU: “Great! Now that you’re oriented, you can start exploring your surroundings. You move around by using the Map Menu at the top left of your screen.” 
MENU: “Navigate over to the scrapyard and check it out!”
MENU: “When you check back in with me. I’ll give you a Spell Card.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “Here you go!”
MENU: “Spell Cards can have different effects. This one restores your lost Stamina. Make sure to use it before you get too tired to move!”
MENU: “You can also recover Stamina by eating the right foods.”
MENU: “Stamina recovers with time, so even if you’re out of items, you’ll be back on your feet after a little while.”
MENU: “Be careful!”

rewards.itemName: Neith's River of Life Spell Card

title: Your First Kami
introDialogue:
MENU: “Now you’re oriented and on your feet, all you need to do is make friends with a Kami yourself!”
MENU: “Have you seen the Vending Machine out in the junkyard?”
MENU: “Believe it or not, we’ve got it powered on and fully stocked!”
MENU: “Don’t worry about how, just go get yourself a Kami.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “Congratulations! You’ve got yourself a Kamigotchi!”
MENU: “Aren’t they just adorable?”

title: What Kami Do: Harvesting
introDialogue:
MENU: “Kamigotchi will rest and stay healthy when being held. Being with an Operator seems to comfort them.”
MENU: “When you set a Kami down, they’ll hang out by the closest spiritual node.”
MENU: “A Kami on a node is considered to be Harvesting, but they’re not working up a sweat. It’s something that happens passively.”
MENU: “When your Kami is out in the world, they draw energy out of the environment and into themselves.”
MENU: “We call this energy $MUSU. I’ll explain more about it later.”
MENU: “You can wander around while your Kami harvest, but don’t forget where you left them! Tired Kami are vulnerable to predators.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “Great! As a reward, here’s another Spell Card!”
MENU: “You can use this one to help your Kamigotchi level up.”

title: What Kami Do: Scavenging
introDialogue:
MENU: “$MUSU isn’t the only thing the Kamigotchi will collect.”
MENU: “Over time, the Harvesting action generates a force that draws objects and items to the Kami.”
MENU: “It’s like a kind of magnetism that only affects certain things.”
MENU: “Which things? It’s not clear. The selection changes based on your Kami’s location. Some are simply fallen sticks and old stones, but other items are more interesting.”
MENU: “We call this action “Scavenging”.”
MENU: “Try it out and see what kind of present the Kami brings you!”
resolutionDialogue: MENU: “Cool find! If you want to know more about an item, just open your inventory and take a closer look.”

title: What Kami Do: Liquidating
introDialogue:
MENU: “If your Kami isn’t enjoying Harvesting, it may not be built for such a relaxed existence.”
MENU: “Kami with a high Violence stat are more content with a life of conflict.”
MENU: “These predatory Kami can use their wild, violent natures to Liquidate other Kami in combat.”
MENU: “Unlike Harvesting, this grants a large bounty of $MUSU at one time.”
MENU: “Give it a shot if you’ve got the Kami for it. I won’t judge!”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “Liquidating other Kami may be antisocial, but there’s no real harm done.”
MENU: “Just like a Kami isn’t really hurt when it’s low on Health, a Kami isn’t really dead when it’s Liquidated.”
MENU: “It may be unable to hold its body in shape for a while, but it can always come back!”
MENU: “You can revive fallen Kami with a Spell Card or some special food items."

title: Making $MUSU
introDialogue:
MENU: “$MUSU is a form of energy that exists everywhere in the universe.”
MENU: “The Kamigotchi World has a particularly high concentration. $MUSU is what keeps everything here tied together like the knots in a big rope net.”
MENU: “Collect a moderate amount of $MUSU, then I’ll explain more."
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “Alright!”
MENU: “In this world and the places that surround it, $MUSU is also a kind of currency.” 
MENU: “If you need a specific item, someone might sell just what you need, for the right price.”
MENU: “You might even get the itch to start trading goods yourself!”

title: Supporting Local Businesses
introDialogue:
MENU: “Go spend some $MUSU just to get the hang of it.”
MENU: “If you need directions, there’s a shop through the door a little north of the vending machine.”
MENU: “I’ve heard it trades some interesting things!”
MENU: “When you’re done shopping, I’ll give you something extra.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “She’s ruthless, huh? Maybe you’ll find a friendlier shop later.”
MENU: “And don’t worry, not everyone is trying to rip you off. Here, take this."

objectives.description: Buy something from any vendor using $MUSU

title: Building a Reputation
introDialogue:
MENU: “This one is a more personal request.”
MENU: “I want to do some repairs. If you just give me the raw materials, I can fabricate the parts I need.”
MENU: “Please give me some Scrap Metal. I’d really appreciate it!”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “When you complete a quest, the rewards vary. Sometimes you get items, but you always gain a little character.”
MENU: “Your profile in the Account tab uses a set of meters to track how others see you.”
MENU: “As the Menu, I’m what you use to interface with the Kamigotchi World.”
MENU: “We’re always hanging out, and I’m as friendly as it gets, so raising your Reputation with me is easy.”
MENU: “I’ll always give between 2 to 6 Reputation for completing quests, but sometimes I might think you deserve more.”

rewards.description: 6x Agency Reputation,1x “Paeon’s Field of Flowers” Spell Card

title: Kamigotchi World and You: Having a Normal One
introDialogue:
MENU: “Every area in the Kamigotchi World has a Type just like your Kami.”
MENU: “The “Normal” type is kind of self-explanatory.”
MENU: “If I had to really break it down…”
MENU: “Something with the Normal Type represents the essence of this realm in its purest form.”
MENU: “To get a better idea of what that means, scavenge three items in rooms with the Normal Type.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “You could also think of the Normal type as “Natural” or “Pure”, but those aren’t really right.”
MENU: “It’s just normal for whatever it is.”
MENU: “When something isn’t influenced by any external factors, that’s normal.”

objectives.description: Make a Scavenging roll in 3 rooms of the Normal type

rewards.description: 4x Agency Reputation,1x "Neith’s River of Life" Spell Card

title: Kamigotchi World and You: That Eerie Feeling
introDialogue:
MENU: “The “Eerie” type comes into being when the normal state of things gets changed by something divine or supernatural.”
MENU: “To understand more about this, scavenge three items in rooms with the Eerie type."
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “If you’re not convinced, you’re not alone.”
MENU: “It is pretty eerie, but look at it rationally. Maybe it’s about convincing yourself.”
MENU: “Like when someone believes they’ve witnessed a miracle, or has a nightmare they think was prophetic.”
MENU: “It’s not always clear.”

objectives.description: Make a Scavenging roll in 3 rooms of the Eerie type

title: Kamigotchi World and You: Insects in the Miasma
introDialogue:
MENU: “The Insect type might make your skin crawl, but it’s not always about seeing bugs.”
MENU: “It’s the kind of thing that makes you feel dirty inside and out.”
MENU: “If you want to understand what I mean, scavenge three items in rooms with the Insect type.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “See, the insects are more of a symbol.”
MENU: “They thrive in places where the natural environment is corrupted or defiled.”
MENU: “Centipedes are attracted to places where this corruption is particularly intense.”

objectives.description: Make a Scavenging roll in 3 rooms of the Insect type

title: Kamigotchi World and You: Tossed in the Scrap Heap
introDialogue:
MENU: “Like they say, one person’s trash is another person’s treasure!”
MENU: “I shouldn’t call it trash, though.”
MENU: “A Scrap Type isn’t defined as waste material that has been discarded, but as a small part of a greater whole.”
MENU: “Scavenge three items in rooms with the Scrap type and see what you find.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “People have the power to make tangible changes in the world.
MENU: “When they manifest will, create attachments with desire, and leave behind memories, they make a mark.”
MENU: “The remnants of those material attachments can end up as Scrap.”
MENU: “And that’s every type covered!”
MENU: “Please remember: Your Kami’s Type will affect how quickly they harvest $MUSU on a given node.”
MENU: “Maximize your gains by playing to their advantages!”

objectives.description: Make a Scavenging roll in 3 rooms of the Scrap type

rewards.description: 4x Agency Reputation,1x "Neith’s River of Life" Spell Card

title: Identifying Materials: Wood
introDialogue:
MENU: “You probably picked up a Wooden Stick or two while out scavenging with your Kami.”
MENU: “Well, here’s a rumor: These trees are all branches of one huge tree, connected by the roots!”
MENU: “That’s why all the Wooden Sticks you’ll find are pretty much the same, no matter where you pick them up.”
MENU: “Is it true? I don’t know. I could analyze the wood’s structure in detail.”
MENU: “Collect 5 Wooden Sticks for me and I’ll look into it.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “Yep. It’s wood.”
MENU: “These are in fact identical, not “pretty much” the same.”
MENU: “Exact down to the molecules. Are my sensors broken?”
MENU: “Whatever. They’re just sticks.”

title: Identifying Materials: Stone
introDialogue:
MENU: “Sometimes a rock isn’t just a rock. Sometimes it really “rocks”!”
MENU: “…”
MENU: “I’m sorry.”
MENU: “What I meant to say is: The structure of these rocks doesn’t always make geological sense.”
MENU: “It’s natural for them to be a little different. The Kamigotchi World doesn’t follow the same laws as the physical world.”
MENU: “But I want to know exactly how they differ. Collect 5 Rocks so I can run some tests.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “This is really something.”
MENU: “A couple of these rocks can’t be matched to a known type.”
MENU: “Hard to believe, but they fracture and shear like bone.”
MENU: “What kind of geological processes make a rock like that?”
MENU: “You don’t need to answer. Just thinking out loud.”

title: Identifying Materials: Metal
introDialogue:
MENU: “I’d like to do some more work on myself.”
MENU: “Get me 5 pieces of Scrap Metal, please. Just like last time.”
MENU: “And keep your fingers crossed.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “That should do the trick. Thanks a lot!”
MENU: “...”
MENU: “No. There are still so many empty spaces.”
MENU: “Don’t get worried about me, but I feel like I’m missing something important.”
MENU: “Ah well. I’ll figure it out.”

title: Exploring Other Options
introDialogue:
MENU: “I didn’t get what I wanted last time, but it’s better than I thought.”
MENU: “A cross-check of all available data led me to discover this world is much bigger than it seems.”
MENU: “I’ll collect data and look for hidden paths as you move around.”
MENU: “Once I know where they are, maybe I can help open them up.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “Quite the workout, no? Here, this spell card will perk you up.”
MENU: “That huge lab facility was an obvious place to explore, but it’s locked up tight.”
MENU: “Where else to go? Under the water?”
MENU: “I’ll think about it.”

rewards.description: 2x Agency Reputation,1x "Neith’s River of Life" Spell Card

title: Harvesting Data I
introDialogue:
MENU: “Please hike to the deepest part of the scrapyard and check it out for me.”
MENU: “I think something is buried behind all that scrap.”
MENU: “All that strange machinery piled up in one place…”
MENU: “It might be covering up something even more interesting.”
MENU: “Harvest for at least 12 hours in the Scrap Confluence so I can run an extended analysis.”
MENU: “If I find anything, I’ll let you know.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “Just looks like a bunch of nonsense to me.”
MENU: “Everything here is supposed to warp you through dimensions or something like that.”
MENU: “But it’s clearly all junk that doesn’t work.”
MENU: “I don’t think we’ll find anything useful in this mess."

objectives.description: Harvest for >720 minutes in Scrap Confluence

rewards.description: 4x Agency Reputation,1x “Paeon’s Field of Flowers” Spell Card

title: Harvesting Data II
introDialogue:
MENU: “If we want to find out how to get inside the Labs, we should look for clues.”
MENU: “Harvest for at least 12 hours at the Labs Entrance while I put my mind to this.”
MENU: “Even if we can’t brute force the doors open right away, I’m sure we can find out where the key is hiding.”
MENU: “If not, I guess we need another approach.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “Forget about finding a clue to where the key is. I can’t even find the keyhole.”
MENU: “This place is sealed up like the pyramids.”
MENU: “Someone really doesn’t want us to go this way.”

objectives.description: Harvest for >720 minutes at Labs Entrance

rewards.description: 4x Agency Reputation,2x “Paeon’s Field of Flowers” Spell Card

title: Harvesting Data III
introDialogue:
MENU: “What if there’s something behind that waterfall?”
MENU: “Go to the Hollow Path and Harvest for at least 12 hours.”
MENU: “The Temple by the Waterfall doesn’t have a Node, so that’s the closest place you can set Kami down.”
MENU: “I’ll run my tests from there. We’ll find some answers soon.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “Good news!”
MENU: “I’ve confirmed the existence of a cave beyond the waterfall.”
MENU: “Unfortunately, I don’t think you can just jump inside.”
MENU: “I don’t mean to be rude, but you seem to abhor running water.”
MENU: “There has to be a way to get us in.”

objectives.description: Harvest for >720 minutes at the Hollow Path

rewards.description: 4x Agency Reputation,1x “Paeon’s Field of Flowers” Spell Card,1x “Melkarth’s Heroic Awakening” Spell Card

title: Squaring the Circle I
introDialogue:
MENU: “The holes in my memory are frustrating.”
MENU: “I should know how to progress, but it’s like someone wanted me to forget.”
MENU: “Bear with me for a while, okay? I have one more thing to try.”
MENU: “A long, thorough, final search around the map, clockwise from the top.”
MENU: “We’ll find something.”
MENU: “Start at Scrap Trees and dig around. I’ll connect to your Kami and share what they learn.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “I thought we’d find something juicy in the notebook, but the writing is just weird poetry.”
MENU: “Not much of it either. It’s more of a sketchbook, really.”
MENU: “Most of the drawings are pictures of the same young woman.”
MENU: “She looks kind of familiar, but I can’t place her.”
MENU: “The rest of them look like monsters…” 
MENU: “Some of them are kind of scary, but others look cute. They kind of resemble the Kamigotchi.”
MENU: “The word “Moon” was written on the inside cover. Does that mean something to you?”

objectives.description: Make 2 Scavenging rolls at Scrap Trees

title: Squaring the Circle II
introDialogue:
MENU: “Continue moving clockwise around the map.”
MENU: “Next up, visit Centipedes and send your Kami out to scavenge.”
MENU: “Is there something in that hole? It seems dangerous there.”
MENU: “The insects really thrive in that part of the woods. More than most places.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “It’s disgusting.”
MENU: “Something under there is exceedingly rotten.”
MENU: “It’s so far gone that I can’t tell what it was originally. Just a carpet of worms now.”
MENU: “I would have preferred to find some hidden treasure.”
MENU: “Nothing else to do here for now.”

title: Squaring the Circle III
introDialogue:
MENU: “We haven’t gotten answers yet, but we’ve made a little progress. 
MENU: “More information helps. More context helps.”
MENU: “Even if we don’t find something with a direct connection to the labs, I feel like we’re on the right track.”
MENU: “Next, visit the Blooming Tree and make scavenging rolls.”
MENU: “The weather here is always clear. It wasn’t lightning, so what blasted that tree apart?
MENU: “It probably won’t open any doors for us, but I’m still curious.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “Didn’t come up with anything useful. That tree is still a mystery.”
MENU: “It’s got a certain presence, that’s for sure.”
MENU: “I thought it was dead, but it’s still hanging on.”
MENU: “It’s probably one of the oldest things in this place. My circuits might corrode before it grows back, though.”
MENU: “Interesting, but not what we’re looking for. Let’s keep going.”

objectives.description: Make 3 Scavenging Rolls at Blooming Tree

title: Squaring the Circle IV
introDialogue:
MENU: “Visit the Airplane Crash and scavenge around with your Kami.”
MENU: “I haven’t mentioned that plane until now, but that’s because I have no clue where it came from or what it’s doing here.
MENU: “I’m supposed to have pretty thorough data for this area!”
MENU: “It’s not a gap I feel an absence from, this thing is a genuine foreign body.”
MENU: “What’s the story? Who was on it? Where did they go? Could we follow them?”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “A small private jet with one pilot and four passengers. 
MENU: “They drew a primitive representation of their party on the interior roof of the plane.”
MENU: “Later, something drove them away from this place. The last thing they left was this journal.”
MENU: “If we find any of these people hanging around, you might want to talk to them.”
MENU: “Still, I don’t think pursuing this story will help us pass the waterfall. We should move on.”

objectives.description: Make 3 Scavenging Rolls at Airplane Crash

title: Squaring the Circle V
introDialogue:
MENU: “Visit the Clearing and do a little scavenging. Do your best to not disturb the cairn.”
MENU: “Mounds of stone like this are often meant as burial mounds.
MENU: “So who or what is buried here?”
MENU: “With every dead end we lose another lead and pile on more junk data.”
MENU: “Let’s hope this one has something useful.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “That’s strange. There’s absolutely nothing here.”
MENU: “I don’t feel good about this search as a whole. We’re turning up so much information, but it’s all irrelevant.”
MENU: “I apologize for being so cold regarding the passengers of that private jet.”
MENU: “I’m just focused on something else.”
MENU: “It’s time to get moving.”

title: Squaring the Circle VI
introDialogue:
MENU: “Number 6. Go to the Labs Entrance one more time and make scavenging rolls.”
MENU: “Why is that building sealed so well?”
MENU: “That really bothers me. When we get the doors open, I’m going to have so many questions ready.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “Someone’s definitely inside, and they definitely locked us out on purpose.”
MENU: “I don’t know why they’d do that, though.”
MENU: “Aren’t you supposed to go this way?“
MENU: “I managed to get a list of users that passed through these doors. I’m still making the connections, but one of the names…”
MENU: “I think we’re really close to finding something. I just remembered where to look next.”
MENU: “Take a handful of Booster Packs. I put you through a real grind, so I hope you get lucky.”

objectives.description: Make 9 Scavenging Rolls at Labs Entrance

title: A Forgotten Friend
introDialogue:
MENU: “Let’s cut the boundary pushing. I remembered something.”
MENU: “Go to the Lost Skeleton and make scavenging rolls. Let’s give the place a thorough check.”
MENU: “That skeleton has a business laptop. That’s definitely an employee from the labs!”
MENU: “Something’s been making me overlook him until now, but I won’t miss anything this time.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “A data chip?! This is a goldmine!”
MENU: “Keep that thing safe until we can read it!”
MENU: “We can’t read it yet, but I’m not about to let something like that hold us back.”
MENU: “The laptop’s battery is missing. I can’t read the chip without a device to interface with. 
MENU: “These are pretty big problems, but compared to the ones we had before? Light work.”
MENU: “Thanks for doing this with me.”

objectives.description: Make 5 Scavenging Rolls at Lost Skeleton

rewards.description: 6x Agency Reputation,1x Booster Pack,"KW Maps" Data Chip

title: Deeper Understanding
introDialogue:
MENU: “Try the Scrap Confluence for something that might be able to read a data chip.”
MENU: “There’s so much tech there, some of it is bound to work.”
MENU: “Fingers crossed you find a laptop battery.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “You know, some of this stuff would be really interesting if it weren’t all broken crap.”
MENU: “I compiled some notes. I don’t think they’re relevant, but you might find it fun to read.”
MENU: “This was a failure, but I feel like it was a good try.”
MENU: “The next place is bound to work!”

objectives.description: Make 2 Scavenging Rolls at Scrap Confluence

title: Computer Blues
introDialogue:
MENU: “Try the Marketplace room for anything that might be able to read a data chip.”
MENU: “That’s the only working computer around here, I think.”
MENU: “Just go buy something and look for an SD card port while you’re doing it.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “Another failure.”
MENU: “It was a longshot, but I really thought that machine might have had a functional data port.”
MENU: “It doesn’t even have USB!”
MENU: “We might need to resort to drastic measures.”

objectives.description: Use the machine to buy something in the Marketplace

title: Humility
introDialogue:
MENU: “You know that strange woman here, near the entrance?”
MENU: “I’ve learned her name is Mina by following along with you. Go to her shop and ask her for help.”
MENU: “Mina is kind of scary. She’s also completely foreign to my databases, like that crashed jet. But Mina is alive.”
MENU: “And independent. And powerful!”
MENU: “She’s been giving you quests. I can read them too, you know.”
MENU: “That means she can take control over parts of my interface… It’s not nice.”
MENU: “On the other hand, this also makes it more likely that she can help.”
MENU: “What would be a nice welcoming gift for someone like that? Flowers?”
MENU: “No, I’ve got the perfect idea. When you visit on my behalf, just fold a big stack of $MUSU in the greeting card.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “Thanks. We started to talk a little bit, behind the scenes.”
MENU: “She was very surprised to learn that I exist. Like as a person.”
MENU: “We could have put our heads together earlier, but I guess our personalities didn’t allow for it.”
MENU: “We both trust you, though. That’s doing a lot for me in her eyes.”
MENU: “Mina and I both seem to have an incomplete picture of what’s going on here, so we’ll try to help each other learn the details.”
MENU: “She seems to respect me now that she knows I exist, and she apologized for using me without permission.”
MENU: “I think she’s a nice enough old lady. Don’t tell her I said that.”

title: Pyramid Power
introDialogue:
MENU: “With that item from Mina, you should be able to power up that laptop.”
MENU: ”So go do it! Fire that baby up!”
MENU: ”I want to see what’s on that chip!”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “That confirms my first scans. There’s a large system of caves hidden behind the waterfall.”
MENU: ”I remember now.”
MENU: ”These documents describe the Kamigotchi World in detail, even parts we’ve never seen.”
MENU: ”The labs aren’t mentioned. They’re as foreign as the plane is.”
MENU: ”I must remember them from somewhere else.”
MENU: ”I’m sure I was inside once, a long time ago.”
MENU: ”Talk to Mina about passing the water. She might have some ideas.”

objectives.description: Give Pyramid Engine (Soulbound),Move to Lost Skeleton

title: Safe Hex
introDialogue:
MINA: “That was a mean prank on my part, but I knew you could handle it.”
MINA: ”Water can be an effective barrier for spirits.”
MINA: ”I thought, strange thing that you are, that you might make it. Alas, no luck.”
MINA: ”Lucky for you, as a goddess of all that flows, I can help out with the waterfall.”
MINA: “This kind of thing might stay beyond your reach for a while, but I can simply do it for you.”
MINA: ”Collect the proper reagents for me and I will cast a charm that will let you walk under flowing water.”
MINA: ”It’ll practically last forever.”
MINA: ”We’ll start with something that has a sticky and water-resistant nature.”
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “To pass under flowing water is one thing, but to be submerged can be a difficult and dangerous experience for those who aren’t watery by nature.”
MINA: “Don’t even think about trying to go swimming in ponds and rivers.”
MINA: “At best, you’ll walk on the surface like solid land.”
MINA: “At worst, you’ll go under and never come back up.”
MINA: “Someone like you might be able to explore underwater with a magic ring or some charm like that.”
MINA: ”Anyway, you’re distracting me.”
MINA: “Bring the rest of the ingredients before the resin sets.”
MENU: “Please be patient with them!”
MENU: “Mina, they’re trying their best…”

rewards.description: 2x Agency Reputation,2x Elders Loyalty,1x Booster Pack

title: Holier Than Thou
introDialogue:
MINA: “Next, some Holy Syrup.”
MINA: “The holy syrup will help form a barrier against the purifying effects of flowing water.”
MINA: “It also makes the potion spread more smoothly.”
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “How does this work? Well, an ablution can’t purify what’s already pure.”
MINA: “Something like that.”
MINA: “Don’t fret about the logic behind magic, it’s not a hard science.”
MINA: “Symbols, will, spirits, enchantments, it’s all a rather loose thing. 
MINA: “Just find two more ingredients and I’ll mix and apply the hex.”
MINA: “And trust me, it will work.”

title: Taking Great Pains
introDialogue:
MINA: “The third ingredient I’ll need is some Black Poppy Extract.”
MINA: “This one will be a bit of a struggle, but it’s a very important aspect of the hex.”
MINA: “I’ll explain why you need the extract after you get back with it.”
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “The rest of the ingredients will protect against the substantial part of the damage.”
MINA: “Even so, the pain you experience would still be intense.”
MINA: “This will help mitigate the pain as you cross under the waterfall.”
MINA: “Anything should be easier to tolerate with a little poppy extract.”

title: Steel Your Heart
introDialogue:
MINA: “Last, we’re going to need a large amount of Scrap Metal.”
MINA: “No, it’s not for a big container.”
MINA: “I’ll answer questions after you give me the materials.”
MINA: “Aren’t we supposed to be in a hurry?”
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “This will get broken down and mixed with the other ingredients to form a light metallic coating.”
MINA: “It gives the mixture a lasting permanence.”
MINA: “You’ll never notice it’s there. It’s not like you need to breathe anyway.”

rewards.description: 4x Agency Reputation,4x Elders Loyalty,2x Booster Pack,Unlock Caves

title: The Sanctuary Caves
introDialogue:
MINA: “I’ll be scrying you while you explore. I’m not coming in myself, but I’ll be watching closely.”
MINA: “I can still send you messages through Menu when I need favors done.”
MENU: “Between you and me, Mina seems excited.” 
MENU: She doesn’t show it like I do, but when it comes to not knowing the whole story, she gets just as frustrated. 
MINA: “I just said I’d be scrying you. That means watching and listening.” 
MINA: “But yes. I’m hoping to get some of my own questions answered inside.”
MENU: “For me, it’s personal. I used to know things and someone took them away from me.”
MENU: “Not only do I want to restore my data on the caves, I want to know who did this to me and why.”
MENU: ”Then you can have the Kami eat them, I guess.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “This place…”
MENU: “I’ll have to talk to you later.”
MENU: “Sorry.”
MENU: “I’m processing a lot.”

objectives.description: Use the object "Waterfall" in "Temple by the Waterfall" to enter a new zone

rewards.description: 4x Agency Reputation,4x Elders Loyalty,1x Booster Pack

title: Into the Depths
introDialogue:
MENU: “It looks like Mina’s trick worked. My database access just expanded to include the new zone.”
MENU:  “I still don’t have a complete picture, but the more you explore, the more I seem to remember.”
MENU: “Those little bald statues, this temple behind the waterfall, it’s all so familiar.”
MENU: ”The key to unlocking the labs has to be back here somewhere, wherever here is.”
MENU: “We won’t know more until we collect more data, so you know what to do.”
MENU: “Get your kami harvesting and let's see what turns up.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “It’s almost like these caves are trying to confuse me.”
MENU: “If this is supposed to be some sort of ancient temple, I certainly don't recognize the culture it came from.”
MENU: “And there’s so much ozone in the air.”
MENU: “I can feel the electricity.”
MENU: “If I could still have headaches, I would definitely have one right now.”
MENU: “...”
MENU: “Maybe Mina will have some ideas.”

objectives.description: Harvest for >720 minutes at Temple Cave

title: Feeling in the Dark
introDialogue:
MINA: “I was just thinking. At long last, I got you to take a shower.”
MINA: “...”
MENU: “...”
MINA: “...”
MENU: “Mina please.”
MINA: “Too much? Fine, fine.”
MINA: “Let me get my bearings through the Operator’s eyes, one moment.”
MINA: “Ah, Menu, I can see where your headache came from.”
MINA: “Those magicians treated the sacred grounds of your distant forebears like free real estate.”
MENU: “I don’t know if we’re related.”
MINA: “In the unlikely chance that you do manage to open up the labs, I will have words for the scoundrels.”
MINA: “For now, let us investigate the depths of this desecration.”
MINA: “Operator, have your kami scavenge around and we’ll see what they have wrought.”
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “Hm! Hm! Hm! Yes, I see.”
MINA: “I'm somewhat familiar with the nature of the resources you’ll gather here."
MINA: “However, I sense a rare energy in the air.”
MINA: “It only lingers here near the mouth of the cave, but if we could go deeper…”
MINA: This place could be very useful. Keep looking around."

objectives.description: Make 7 Scavenging Rolls at Temple Cave

rewards.description: 2x Agency Reputation,2x Elders Loyalty,1x Booster Pack

title: Where It Stems From
introDialogue:
MENU: “The mushrooms found in these caves are somewhat different from those found on the surface.”
MENU: “I don't have much data on them, but try scavenging some with your kami anyway, maybe they'll be useful for something."
resolutionDialogue: MENU: Well, they're not edible, and they're not even psychoactive, how lame!

title: Better Than Chopping Wood?
introDialogue:
MENU: “Okay, you might not be able to trip off those little mushrooms, or eat them at all for that matter, but they’re still neat!”
MINA: “They’re hard and solid, like a dense wood.”
MINA: “Get a pile of stems and glue them together and you could make some lumber.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “There you go! 
MENU: “This lumber could be very useful for something. I’m just not sure what.”
MINA: “Maybe you could make a battering ram and break open the door to the labs with it.”
MENU: “Ha, I wish.”
MENU: “Keep looking around for now, we’ll save the property crime for later.”

title: Throw Me a Bone!
introDialogue:
MENU: “There has to be a logical reason for this world to be like this.”
MENU: “Even if we’ve gotta tear out all our existing notions of reality and start from scratch, there still have to be answers and we can find them.”
MENU: “A scientist wouldn't just give up on understanding the world because it threw them a curveball like pipes fossilized in Jurassic bedrock.”
MENU: “There are always answers and the scientific method can lead us to them.”
MENU: “Keep scavenging around inside the caves with your Kami, we need more data.”
MENU: “We’ve got to observe the environment so that we can orient ourselves to it.
MENU: “So get out there and do some observing!”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “Whoa! That isn't some fossilized dinosaur bone, it's really huge.”
MENU: “This came from a creature of truly titanic proportions.”
MENU: “We'd better hope you don't run into a living one.”
MENU: “It's super sturdy though! Could be a good construction material.”
MENU: “I doubt its former owner will miss it much.”

title: Learning From a Master
introDialogue:
MINA: “I suppose I can spare some information for you.”
MINA: “See the walls of this room? The large blocks of stone are smooth cut and fit together like the pieces of a puzzle.”
MINA: “It appears almost natural, but shows the great skill of the master craftsman.”
MINA: “You should have all the ingredients you need to practice.”
MINA: “Just grind up a bone chunk to make mortar and use it to fit your stones and bones together”
MINA: “It's a bit old fashioned, but it’s strong and long lasting.”
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “Hmm. That looks…”
MINA: “It’s fine.”
MINA: “Maybe you were more of a bricklayer than a mason in a past life.”
MINA: “Not everyone can be talented, skilled, and beautiful all the time. You’ll get better.”

title: Sound of One Hand Clapping
introDialogue:
MINA: “Head over to that temple by the entrance, and quit gawking at the scenery.”
MINA: “Most of what you’ll find in here was deposited by the flow of $MUSU.”
MINA: “However, this flow is far from uniform.”
MINA: “It’s more like a mountain river winding its way through the rocks, like that waterfall outside.”
MINA: “As it flows through these realms, pools and eddies are created as the current encounters obstacles in its path.”
MINA: “Inside these pools of $MUSU you can find not only the echoes of physical relics, but condensations of things which, within your world, exist only as concepts or abstractions.”
MINA: “Memorable dreams or intense nightmares, powerful emotions, or even raw physical sensations such as touch, sight, or sound.”
MINA: “That stuff can make for a really powerful reagent.”
MINA: “There’s a swirl in the currents like that in this room, and so this is a good place to scavenge some.”
MINA: “What are you waiting for? You won’t learn magic if you just keep standing there.”
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “Wow, you really did it?”
MINA: “I didn’t think you would succeed so quickly. This stuff is pretty difficult to get your hands on.”
MINA: “As you’ve probably noticed, this is a physical material made of the sensation of hearing.”
MINA: “Haven’t seen that one before, huh?”
MINA: “There are six sensory essences like that in total. Think you'll be lucky enough to find them all here?”

title: Mystery Machines
introDialogue:
MENU: “The cute little temple by the entrance doesn’t lead anywhere else within the cave system, but it might have some good leads for our investigation.”
MENU: “The machines there are super weird.  And really, really old.”
MENU: “I see equipment from the labs plugged into it though. It's obviously connected somehow. We might find a clue how to get inside the building.”
MENU: “Let me scan the room and see what I can find.”
MENU: “Do some harvesting in the meantime, it usually helps me think.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “the only thing on the monitors is static and the internal “logic” of their equipment is like something out of a fever dream; it’s all black boxes with nonsense circuitry.”
MENU: “It’s probably some kind of quantum tech, maybe we’ll find a book explaining it when we get into the labs.”
MENU: “Sorry I didn’t find anything useful, but wait…what’s that?”
MENU: “Another chip!”

objectives.description: Harvest for >720 minutes at the Techno Temple

title: Can’t Stop With Just One
introDialogue:
MENU: “This could be big! That datachip must have some juicy info on it.”
MENU: “Maybe it’s a journal or diary from someone important.
MENU: “Or a book on this crazy tech that I could use to upgrade myself.”
MENU: “This could be the breakthrough I’m looking for!”
MENU: “Quick, connect that datachip to the old laptop and I’ll see what I can pull off of it.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “Oh.
MENU: “I see.”
MENU: “This contains blueprint instructions for designing and assembling structures in the Kamigotchi world using local materials.”
MENU: “That’s… Pretty cool?”
MENU: “Neither you nor your Kami really need to sleep, but it’ll be nice to have a place for your stuff.”
MENU: “Still, I was really hoping for something more significant.”
MENU: “They could at least have given me a cool book to read. I’d love to find out more about these machines.”

title: Sweet As Honey
introDialogue:
MENU: “You can find these glittering candies growing on mushroom stalks down here.”
MENU: “They’ll probably show up in a few other places, too.”
MENU: “It’s not like Big Rock Candy Mountain, the candy doesn’t just show up on its own.”
MENU: “The Honeydew Scale is actually a small insect native to dark environments like this cave.”
MENU: “The candy is like a protective shell that builds up over time on their backs.”
MENU: “This has nothing to do with my mission to get inside the labs, but it’s really neat!”
MENU: “Besides, it’s handy that you can find them here. They look like a great natural food source for your Kami.”
MENU: “Let’s rest here for a bit and let your Kami scavenge some.”
MENU: “Take in the sights while I try to figure out where we should go next.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “Honeydew Scale is a delicious and renewable food source, but it’s not always easy to find.”
MENU: “We’re pretty lucky to find so many of them in one place.”

title: Ringing Any Bells I
introDialogue:
MINA: “Hey. You.”
MINA “While Menu is doing her scans, can you examine that bell hanging above the crossroads for me?”
MINA: “It doesn’t appear to match the age or style of the decor we’ve seen in prior rooms.”
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “Aha! Right as usual.”
MINA: “This bell is much more recent than the rest of the architecture.”
MINA: “It seems to be Iron Age, maybe Bronze, it all kind of blurs together after a while.”
MINA:  “I suppose that still isn’t very recent for you.”
MINA: “Anyway, that doesn’t matter, because it wasn’t hung here anywhere near when it was made.”
MINA: “Based on the way it’s anchored to the cave roof, I’d guess it was placed here within the last few decades.”
MINA:  “That would make it the work of our magician friends, but I’ll need to see more before determining what they were doing with it.”

objectives.description: Harvest for >720 minutes at Cave Crossroads

title: Pipe Dream
introDialogue:
MENU: “In your world, there’s this concept called the law of superposition.” 
MENU: “It says that the rocks at the bottom are older than the rocks at the top and that stuff was laid down in sequential layers.”
MENU: “Even if those layers get tipped or flipped or something, there’s an original stacking order.”
MENU: “The pipes running through the walls kinda break that rule though. They’re actually older than the rock itself.”
MINA: “That might be a result of some of the magic left behind from the original inhabitants of this place. Hard to say.”
MINA: “If you scavenge some of the pipes, we might be able to tell you more.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “My careful analysis has determined these pipes are probably the oldest things we’ve seen so far.”
MENU: “They’re super sturdy and protected from decay by that lustrous tarnish.”
MINA: “It’s like they’re being used as reinforcing bars for the stone.”
MINA: “If that’s not natural rock, it’s concrete to beat the Romans. And with a beautiful natural finish.”
MENU: “Don’t be silly. That doesn’t make any sense.”
MENU: “Anyway. The pipes are solid and rich in iron. We might be able to use this metal.”

title: Community Service
introDialogue:
MINA: “The litter is out of control, even inside of these caves.”
MINA: “At least the literal piles of garbage haven’t made it here yet.”
MINA: “You’re good at menial tasks. Do me a favor and pick up a few of the cigarette butts, at least.”
MENU: “That’s not going to do anything, you know they'll just come back. Don’t you have anything more import-”
MENU: “...”
MENU: “Actually, could you give them to me afterwards? I have an idea.”
MINA: “Please don’t share it with us.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “Exactly as I suspected.”
MENU: “These are a fine source of Microplastics!”
MENU: “You can just grind them up in that Spice Grinder from Mina’s shop.”
MINA: “I should sell you a second one.”
MINA: “You can use it just for cigarettes.”
MINA: “And here, take this card. You might need it after dealing with such filthy things.”

rewards.description: 2x Elders Loyalty,2x Agency Reputation,1x “Melkarth’s Heroic Awakening” Spell Card

title: You Smelt It…
introDialogue:
MINA: “Those pipes don’t seem structurally necessary, so there shouldn’t be any harm in yanking them out and melting them down.”
MINA: “Use the portable burner to do it. It can get hotter than you might think.”
MINA: “It came with a crucible. Don’t worry if you never noticed before.”
MINA: “You can also make ingots from those scrap metal cans you found on the surface.”
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “This is just an ordinary alloy of good quality.”
MINA: “I had hoped that the smelting process would have cooked out some interesting magical residues."
MINA: "Even so, this could be fine building material.”

title: Ringing Any Bells II
introDialogue:
MENU: “Something in the cave crossroads is still ringing a bell. Pardon the joke.”
MENU: “You still have that essence of hearing your Kami scavenged earlier, right?”
MENU: “Mina, do you think it would let me hear things the way I could when I had a body?”
MINA: “An interesting theory dear, I don’t see the harm in testing it.”
MINA: “The operator can always scavenge another essence.”
MINA: “I think you should go for it.”
MENU: “Let’s do it. Operator, get me an Essence of Hearing please, it’s time for some science.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “Thanks!”
MENU: “If the “Magicians” as Mina calls them really hung that bell…”
MENU: “Maybe I’ve heard it ring in the past.”
MENU: “If I hear it ring again, it might awaken some hidden memories.”
MENU: “I’ll hold off on using the Essence until we’re ready to try.”
MENU: “I don’t know how long this unintended effect will last. I wouldn’t want to waste it and make you go scavenge for more.”

rewards.description: 4x Agency Reputation,4x Elders Loyalty,2x Booster Pack

title: Ringing Any Bells III
introDialogue:
MENU: “I’m ready. Time to ring that bell.”
MENU: “It’s pretty big, so make sure you give it a good hard smack.”
MENU: “If I’m going to hear it, I want to really hear it!”
MINA: “Use the biggest rock you can get as a mallet. Make an Ashlar if you have to.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “Ah!”
MENU: “It’s such a lovely sound…”
MENU: “So deeply safe and resonant, and…and I….I remember something…”
MENU: “Hey! I remember something!”
MENU: “It’s…”
MENU: “I was in the labs somewhere, there were people standing around me.”
MENU: “They were above me, so I must have been laying down.”
MENU: “The room was dark. There was incense burning, and that bell was ringing, that exact one.”
MENU: “There were five of them, two women and three men.”
MENU: “They all seem so familiar, I know these people.”
MENU: “I recognize their faces, I just can’t remember who they are.”
MENU: “They were casting a spell on me I think? Some sort of protective charm or good luck ritual.”
MENU: “I never believed in that stuff, but… Now I just don’t like to think about it.”

title: Flash Back
introDialogue:
MENU: “Mina, can you see the paper talismans around the Crossroads?”
MENU: “They might be easy to miss with the naked eye.”
MINA: “Of course. Obviously some sort of magical item or artifact.”
MINA: “Bring one to me.”
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “This looks to be the work of Menu’s magician friends.”
MENU: “Do you think so?”
MINA: “Of course. Look closely. See how it has a little picture of a Kamigotchi?”
MENU: “Sure. Cute little guy.”
MINA: “Those magicians had quite a learned approach with their application of the craft.”
MINA: “It’s a shame that their intellect was matched by what they lacked in wisdom.”
MINA: “This talisman is a clever bit of spellwork, but very lacking in prudence.”
MINA: “It sacrifices a part of the self for the power it grants.”
MINA:  “There are always tradeoffs when it comes to magic, but there are better reagents than one’s own soul.”
MINA: “I wouldn’t retain my stunning good looks if I relied on spells like this.”

rewards.description: 2x Elders Loyalty,2x Agency Reputation,2x Booster Pack

title: Out of the Blue
introDialogue:
MINA: “Are your Kami feeling hungry?”
MINA: “You can always come back to my shop, you know.”
MINA: “I suppose if you’re too lazy to make the trip, I can let you in on some useful information.”
MINA: “These blue flowers that grow on the cave walls are edible.”
MINA: “You won’t be satisfied eating just one, and they aren’t very nutritious.”
MINA: “They are quite plentiful, though. And honestly delightful."
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “See? Practically effortless to acquire.”
MINA: “You’re likely to find more the deeper you go into the caves.”
MINA: “It’s a common flower that grows more easily in the dark.”

title: Stop and Smell the Lotus
introDialogue:
MINA: “In the underground lake at the bottom of these caves, you can find lotus flowers of uncommon size.”
MINA: “Do you see how their blossoms are shaped? Those shapes create eddies in the $MUSU currents.”
MINA: “If you remember your lessons, you’ll know that means they’re a potential source of sensory essences.”
MINA: “It’s pretty strange to find two different essences so close to each other.”
MINA: “Someone probably cultivated them here so they could get the whole set.”
MINA: “Have your Kami scavenge some essence of smell for us, but be careful, it’s a bit volatile.”
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “Not too shabby.”
MINA: “I thought they might all fly away before you managed to catch one.”

title: Feel Like a Mushroom Myself
introDialogue:
MENU: “Wow, the garden down the middle of this cave just keeps going.”
MINA: “These mushrooms are rarely found in such dense numbers.”
MINA: “If you look closely, you can see the second layer already growing in.”
MINA: “It’s a fungus that grows like a weed when fed properly, but it can rarely get what it wants.”
MENU: “My old friends must have cultivated them hoping to block our way. I don’t think that’ll work very well.”
MENU: “Set your Kami harvesting while I take a closer look.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “Being in this place reminded me of something else.”
MENU: “I think I remember one of the people from that ritual being a gardener of some sort.”
MENU: “They always brought the most beautiful flowers and plants into the office.”
MENU: “Maybe they had something to do with all these mushrooms.” 
MENU: “I unfortunately can’t tell you more with my memory as fragmentary and blocked as it is right now.”
MENU: “If you think it’s frustrating, imagine how I must feel, haha!”
MENU: “Haha…”
MENU: “They must have really wanted me out of the loop.”

objectives.description: Harvest for >720 minutes at Thriving Mushrooms

title: Top 10 Smoke Spots in Kamigotchi World
introDialogue:
MENU: “That spot overlooking the pit… It seems like it would be a great place to stop and have a smoke.”
MENU: “I used to love to smoke. It’s one of the things I miss most about your world.”
MENU: “It’s funny that I remember that despite not remembering very much else from my past life.”
MENU: “If I could burn one right now I bet all my memories would come flooding back.”
MENU: “Give me some cigarette butts? Not even for the microplastics.”
MENU: “Just for old time’s sake.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “If I still had a body I would happily smoke the last of those butts.” 
MENU: “If you’ve ever really craved a cigarette and gone digging around in ashtrays for a half smoked cig you’d know how I feel.”
MENU: “Nothing we can really do about that though.”

objectives.description: Move to Slippery Pit,Give 1 Cigarette Butt

title: Flower of the Flock
introDialogue:
MENU: “These blue flowers you find in the caves, they’re so striking.”
MENU: “They look like the butterflies in the fields and woods outside.”
MENU: “Maybe there’s a connection between them?” 
MENU: “You should deliver some to Mina, she can probably tell us more about them.”
MINA: “I’ve already explained how they can be used in a delightful salad.”
MINA: “But you raise an interesting question. I’ll be happy to take a look.”
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “The resemblance is truly uncanny.”
MINA:  “I doubt that’s a coincidence.” 
MINA: “The butterflies themselves are fragments of human souls, that’s what gives them their unique magic properties.”
MINA: “However, these flowers are mere mimics.”
MINA: “I’m not sensing even a trace of soul within them. They’re just flowers.”
MINA: “Perhaps their appearance allows them to attract real souls for pollination?”
MINA: “Or maybe they look like this so that soul predators will eat them and spread their seeds?”
MINA: “I think I would like to study these further, thank you for bringing them to me.”
MINA:  “Menu was right to think I’d find them interesting.”
MINA:  “Since she’s helped me with this and with so many other things, I should help her with something too.”
MINA: “That’s what friends are for is it not?”

rewards.description: 4x Elders Loyalty,2x Agency Reputation,2x Booster Pack

title: Ordinary Intermediate Potion Crafting (Very Boring) I
introDialogue:
MINA: “Hey, shh.” 
MINA: “Be quiet. We don’t want Menu to hear us.”
MINA: “I’m sure that’s impossible considering where this conversation is taking place, but I’m trying.”
MINA: “Menu is more magical than she likes to think, so I put up anti-scrying measures.”
MINA: “They should work as long as she remains unaware of their presence.”
MINA: “It’s time to teach you some intermediate hexerei with a recipe I’ve just invented.”
MINA: “Bring me some cigarette butts from the cave system. I know I think they’re disgusting, but it’s important.”
MINA: “And don’t tell Menu anything, we don’t want to spoil the surprise.”
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “Ahh, perfect! That will do nicely.”
MINA: “This might look like useless smelly trash, but many things are more than they appear.”
MINA: “We just need two more ingredients. They’ll be hard to get, but not impossible.” MINA: “Head down the central passageway of the caves, stop in that fungus garden, and wait for me.”
MINA: “Be quick about it."

title: Ordinary Intermediate Potion Crafting (Very Boring) II
introDialogue:
MINA: “The mushroom garden in the central hall of this cave is adorable.”
MINA: “It seems to have been abandoned recently. A few decades, maybe a few centuries ago at the most.”
MINA: “While the fungus of the underworld has its own uses, I’m more interested in something else.”
MINA: “You can find rare stone flowers hiding among the mushroom patches.”
MINA: “Those flowers are another of the sensory essences. Make sure to have at least one in your pocket for our potion.”
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “Nicely done! Bring it back to my shop, and remember not to say anything to you-know-who.”
MINA: “;)”

title: Ordinary Intermediate Potion Crafting (Very Boring) III
introDialogue:
MENU: “What’s with these random tasks that Mina has you doing?”
MENU: “She won’t tell me anything about it. And I'm having a hard time seeing what you're doing all of a sudden!”
MENU: “Hey, where are you going?”
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “Hey there, Operator. And shoo Menu, this is a private matter.”
MENU: “But I wanna see! What are you working on? Is it a new potion?”
MINA: “Yes. Now please leave us be.”
MENU: “Awww come on, why? Have I not been a good enough menu?”
MINA: “You’re a very good Menu.”
MINA: “I don’t mean to be rude, but your disembodied presence is interfering with a very delicate crafting process.”
MINA: “Once the potion is completed we’ll show you the final product. I promise.”
MENU: “Okay, fine, fine, I’ll wait outside.”

title: Ordinary Intermediate Potion Crafting (Very Boring) IV
introDialogue:
MINA: “Is she gone?”
MINA: “Phew, that was a close one.”
MINA: “Now, observe closely. Even though these butts have been smoked down to stubs, there is still a bit of tobacco left inside them.”
MINA:  “Menu doesn’t have a physical form. Obviously, just collecting the loose tobacco won’t let her actually smoke.”
MINA: “And even with the Essence trick we tried with that bell, it’s too short-lived and singular.”
MINA: “But I’ve got it all worked out now.”
MINA: “Peel the petals of the Essence of Taste and grind them up in your mortar.”
MINA: “They’ll release oil and emulsify into a thin slurry.”
MINA: “Now, fold in the tobacco.”
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “That looks perfect, now we can give it to Menu.”
MINA: “I wish I could see the look on her face…”
MINA: “I wonder what it looks like?”

title: Ordinary Intermediate Potion Crafting (Very Boring) V
introDialogue:
MINA: “Now that we’ve crafted this sacred incense, you’ll need to burn it in a spot where the $MUSU currents can carry the smoke properly.”
MINA: “Head over to the Torii Gate down the road.”
MINA: “The currents there should work nicely and be far enough away that I don’t get any secondhand smoke at my shop.”
MINA: “Once you’re there, burn the paste in your portable burner.”
MINA: “Don't tell Menu what you're doing, she'll figure it out soon enough. ;)”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “Hey what’s that flavor?”
MENU: “Huh? HUHHH? WHAT?!”
MENU: “Oh my gosh. Oh this is so nice I can feel my pixels chilling out.”
MENU: “What did you…? How did you…?”
MENU: “Mina you sneak! So this is what you two were up to.”
MENU: “Gosh this makes me so happy. It really takes me back… This is calling up so many memories.”
MENU: “I remember the first time I ever smoked, I was sitting in an empty lot after skipping school.”
MENU: “The principal had yelled at me for something stupid and I just started crying and ran out of the building.”
MENU: “I ended up in this lot next to a gas station that was full of spring wildflowers. A homeless guy saw me sitting there sobbing and offered me a smoke.”
MENU: “It was nice, the flowers were pretty.”
MENU: “Oh! Oh and the next time I smoked was after the first time I had sex.”
MENU: “I had met up with this guy I met online and uh…well you probably don’t want details, but it wasn’t a great experience.”
MENU: “He fell asleep afterwards and I sat on his porch chainsmoking for like three hours.”
MENU: “Huh, it’s no wonder I was so addicted to them.”
MENU: “I know none of these memories really help you understand the world any better, but they mean a lot to me.” 
MENU: “Thank you, thank you so much.”

objectives.description: Move to Torii Gate,Give Uninteresting Paste

title: Full Flood
introDialogue:
MINA: “The mural on the wall outside of that antediluvian facility.”
MINA: “Do you recognize the story it depicts?”
MINA: “Nearly all cultures have their own version of the tale. It happened many, many times over the years.”
MINA: “Harvest in that room and I shall study the mural. Perhaps this will tell the story in a way we haven’t all heard before.”
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “The people who painted this lived in a society of great wealth and technological advancement.”
MINA: “As a great flood befell them, gods descended from the heavens to assist a chosen few.” 
MINA: “More or less the same story as always. The details change, but the core remains.”
MINA: “You may even remember tales like this from your own history.”
MINA: “More have been forgotten than remembered.”

objectives.description: Harvest for >720 minutes at Flood Mural

title: Old Fiends
introDialogue:
MENU: “There’s a calm underground stream at the bottom of these caves."
MENU: “The little carving on that rock seems familiar to me somehow. I think I recognize the initials.” 
MENU: “Do some harvesting here while I try to remember."
MENU: “That tobacco you gave me earlier is kind of helping me focus.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “I studied that engraved message. It brought some memories flashing back.”
MENU: “I think I actually knew those people.”
MENU: “One of them, I definitely remember her being really scary.”
MENU: “The other one, he thought he was really funny for some reason.”
MENU: “But I don’t think he was all that clever."
MENU: “Still can’t remember their names, though.”

objectives.description: Harvest for >360 minutes at Still Stream

title: First Contact
introDialogue:
MINA: “In the middle of that facility, there’s a huge black door that doesn’t quite match the rest of it.”
MINA: “It’s sealed up tight. I want you to check out the engraving on the face.”
MINA: “Another old story that happened many times over the course of history.”
MINA: “Gods granting knowledge to humanity, advancing their progress.”
MINA: “Harvest some $MUSU while I read this and I’ll tell you about it."
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “Like the great flood, this is another recurring story.”
MINA: “The gods can’t seem to help themselves.”
MINA: “A fascination with the ephemeral, perhaps?”
MINA: “Or possibly, it’s like wanting the best for your pets.”
MINA: “Whatever the motivation, gods have granted knowledge or power to humans  countless times throughout history.”
MINA: “This is just one of many. A mythic world forgotten and left abandoned.”
MINA: “Until recently, it seems.”

objectives.description: Harvest for >360 minutes at Engraved Door

title: Soaked to the Bone
introDialogue:
MENU: “There’s another skeleton in the caves here, crumpled in the water underneath that observation deck.”
MENU: “It could be someone I know. My memories are coming back more easily now.”
MENU: “Let me do some scans and rack my circuits.”
MENU: “Have your kami do some harvesting over there while you wait for me.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “It seems like this guy died within the last few decades. I wonder where he came from?”
MINA: “Why don’t you ask him?”
MENU: “Haha! That’s a good one. Hello, nice to meet you Mr. Skeleton!”
MENU: “What’s your story, Mr. Skeleton?”
MENU: “…”
MENU: “See? I guess he doesn’t want to talk.”

objectives.description: Harvest for >360 minutes at Shabby Deck

title: On the Fringes
introDialogue:
MINA: “Deep in the ancient facility, there’s a hatch that seems to lead nowhere at all, directly into a cave wall.”
MINA: “I suspect this door might be meant to work like the entrance to my shop. I’m intrigued.”
MINA: “Menu, you’re good with machines, would you be a dear and take a look at it?”
MENU: “Sure! I’m on it! Operator, while you’re waiting for us, go ahead and harvest some $MUSU.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “Alright, analysis complete.”
MENU: “These machines are hundreds of thousands of years old and they’re still in pretty good condition after all that time.”
MENU: “Is that crazy or what?”
MENU: “Unfortunately, that’s about all I can tell you about them.”
MENU: “I don’t exactly have access to the manuals, and the internals look like a bad dream. Nothing rational going on in there.”
MENU: “The monitor on the side has a label for Aether Readings, and another meter for something called GEM Fields, whatever those are.”
MINA: “I have some hypotheses.”
MENU: “Well, it’s all completely dead at the moment. And I think it needs some sort of control device, but it doesn’t seem like there’s one connected.”
MENU: “If you wanted to get it working again, you’d have to plug the wires into a new control device and then power it up somehow.”

objectives.description: Harvest for >360 minutes at Hatch to Nowhere

title: The Doors of Perception
introDialogue:
MINA: “There’s a specific flow of $MUSU around that hatch to nowhere.”
MINA: “You should already know what I mean. It’s a fine place to harvest sensory essence.”
MINA: “Evidence is mounting. This whole place has been renovated to encourage the capture of all six senses."
MINA: “Scavenge around with your kami, we’ll see what else turns up.”
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “An Essence of Touch. What a surprise.”
MINA: “With this essence, arranging those stones to block the tunnel would have been a simple task.”
MINA: “There’s no chance for this to be mere good luck. This is turning out to be a very valuable place to gather resources.”
MINA: “I’ll see about writing up some new potion recipes. Stop by my shop later to see what I’ve come up with.”

title: Reach Out And Touch Faith
introDialogue:
MINA: “Something foul took place in the room with that broken tube. The $MUSU in there room feels tainted.”
MINA: “Whatever was contained within those tubes left an unpleasant imprint onto the world around it.”
MINA: “It seems like a bad idea to loiter for too long, but it’s worth investigating.”
MINA: “Harvest some of the room’s $MUSU and I’ll see to figuring out what happened."
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “Those shattered tubes once contained something very powerful.”
MINA: “I shouldn’t go into much detail. You wouldn’t accept it anyway, I think.”
MINA: “The residue of this power lingers within every object in the room.”
MINA:  “It’s strongest in the glass of the shattered tube and the hoses around it, but everything here has been touched.”
MINA: “To capture and use something like that as a power source…”
MINA: “Truly perverse.”

objectives.description: Harvest for >720 minutes at Broken Tube

title: A Turn of the Screw
introDialogue:
MENU: “Hey so, I know Mina told you not to hang out too long in that broken tube room, but…”
MENU: “I can’t sense whatever she’s picking up on and there’s something of interest to us here.” 
MENU: “According to the map data, this room has a chance of dropping a screwdriver.”
MENU: “If you haven’t already grabbed one from the junkyard then this would be a great place to scavenge one.”
MENU: “Even if you don’t find one, give it a shot.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “While you were scavenging, I cross referenced the drop table in this room with the one in the junkyard. It’s the same model screwdriver.”
MENU: “It must have been someone’s favorite.”
MENU: “I wonder who?”

objectives.description: Make 7 Scavenging Rolls at Broken Tube

title: Platforming Game
introDialogue:
MENU: “Even this deep, we’re still picking up trash.”
MENU: “They really got comfortable in here.”
MENU: “You might as well let the Kami poke around.”
MENU: “Maybe you’ll find something better than cigarette butts and stale burgers.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “It must have been a hassle for people to pass through here.”
MENU: “Seems like a real gymnastics course with all that jumping and climbing.”
MENU: “You don’t seem to have any problem with it, though.”

objectives.description: Make 3 Scavenging Rolls at Toadstool Platforms

title: What Lies Beneath
introDialogue:
MINA: “The growth of these fungi… It’s a bit strange.”
MINA: “It’s a very large cavern in breadth and depth. The mushrooms nearly fill it.”
MINA: “They must be getting plenty of sustenance.”
MINA: “Harvest here a while. You’ll get a better sense of the decay.”
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “As expected. Some kind of enormous rot beneath.”
MINA: “No way to know what it is for sure.”
MINA: “The growth is simply too thick.”

objectives.description: Harvest for >360 minutes at Toadstool Platforms

title: Operator Goes To Camp
introDialogue:
MINA: “Go on. Get moving.”
MINA: “Menu tells me there’s a campsite ahead.” 
MINA: “You’re bound to find a clue left behind, the way these scoundrels litter.”
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “A complete mess, as expected.”
MINA: “Not much of interest to me.”
MINA: “That charcoal drawing on the wall, though…”
MINA: “Have you seen anything like that before?”

title: Digging Up Clues
introDialogue:
MENU: “For litterbugs, they sure cleaned up anything that could trace them.”
MENU: “Nothing to scavenge but the usual stuff.”
MINA: “The blue flowers and mushrooms suggest magical practice.”
MINA: “You might as well dig up some reagents while you’re here.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “Really nothing new, huh.”
MENU: “Wait a minute.”
MENU: “Did you notice the tent?”
MENU: “That symbol looks familiar.”

objectives.description: Make 6 Scavenging Rolls at Abandoned Campsite

title: Get Some Fresh Air
introDialogue:
MENU: “Do me a favor and leave the caves for a minute.”
MENU: “Head back to the Restricted Area and take a look at that office complex.”
MENU: “This might be a pain, but I want you to make sure.”
MENU: “You can keep exploring and put this off for now.”
MENU: “Just keep it in mind.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “Definitely the same logo. It looks like the letter A.”
MENU: “Wonder what it stands for?”

title: Yep, That’s Wood
introDialogue:
MENU: “Check out the construction on these supports.”
MENU: “Wood, yeah. But it’s like that stuff you make out of sticks and stems.”
MENU: “You might as well grab a few more while you’re here.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “So, the wood.”
MENU: “It’s like your laminated timber, but it’s not exactly.”
MENU: “Looks more like chopped up office furniture. Desks, tables.”
MENU: “Why do that?”
MENU: “There are plenty of resources around to make this stuff.”
MENU: “Why haul it in from somewhere else?”

objectives.description: Make 3 Scavenging Rolls at Reinforced Tunnel

title: Plasmatics 101
introDialogue:
MINA: “Have you seen that book leaning against the pillar?”
MINA: “Menu seems to have no interest.”
MINA: “She takes fascination in the strangest things.”
MINA: “I’m always happy to find some new magical text, however.”
MINA: “Have your Kami spend some time harvesting here while I negotiate with our mutual friend.”
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “Apparently she’s already got the contents recorded somewhere.”
MINA: “For some reason it’s been difficult to show these files to you.”
MINA: “She’ll figure it out eventually.”
MINA: “Oh, the book? Nonsense. Foolish fancy. I didn’t finish it."

objectives.description: Harvest for >360 minutes at Reinforced Tunnel

title: Fringe Theories
introDialogue:
MENU: “I took a look at that “Plasmatics” book with Mina.”
MENU: “Most of it is stuff I’m not really familiar with.
MENU: “The parts I am familiar with, I can kind of tell it’s bullshit.”
MENU: “But then there’s this other part that I can’t get a handle on.”
MENU: “You mind going around and helping me compare it with the tech here?”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “I think I’m getting a better vocabulary for this stuff, at least.”
MENU: “Gravitoelectromagnetism as a unified field, zero point vacuum energy production…”
MENU: “It’s starting to remind me of that stuff Mina told me about the aether.”
MENU: “The technobabble feels better than the retro superstition, at least.”

title: The Great Divide
introDialogue:
MENU: “That bridge looks like fake wood.”
MENU: “Check if it’s sturdy by sending some Kami out on it.”
MENU: “I’m just kidding!”
MENU: “Get them harvesting, though.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “I wonder how you were meant to cross before the bridge got built?”
MINA: “Maybe you weren’t.”
MINA: “It may have another purpose.”

objectives.description: Harvest for >360 minutes at Canyon Bridge

title: Edge of the Abyss
introDialogue:
MINA: “There’s an abundance of the blue pansies to be found around that canyon.”
MINA: “It usually means an abundance of magic.”
MINA: “Scavenge around and snatch a few up, let’s have a closer look.”
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “Hmm…”
MINA: “Be wary, Operator. Of whatever you find at the bottom.”

objectives.description: Make 3 Scavenging Rolls at Canyon Bridge

title: Blue Light Special
introDialogue:
MINA: “I don’t need to give you the lesson about $MUSU currents again.”
MINA: “As predicted, you’ll find another essence here.”
MINA: “It seems you can’t touch that lantern. No great worry.”
MINA: “A bit strange, but no obstacle.” 
MINA: “As long as you and your Kami can scavenge, you’re gold.”
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “That lantern burns with a familiar element.”
MINA: “The lingering radiation in the antediluvian facility, you remember?”
MINA: “In a way, that tube was really just a bigger lantern.”
MINA: “Consider yourself lucky being unable to touch it.”
MINA: “Just extract the essence from the light it casts on the wall.”

objectives.description: Make 1 Scavenging Roll at Geometric Cliffs

title: Vision of Life
introDialogue:
MINA: “Make sure to get the essence in this room.”
MINA: “Of all six, Sight is one of the most prized.”
MINA: “The power of a true vision is worth much.”
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “Hold onto one after you’ve got it.”
MINA: “I’m thinking of an interesting use for it.”

title: Soot and Ash
introDialogue:
MENU: “This really stands out. It’s beautiful.”
MENU: “Except for the burnt pages of some book all over the floor.”
MENU: “They must have used that for the carbon. Passionate about their art.”
MENU: “Burn up scraps are gonna be hard to decipher. I’ll work on it.”
MENU: “The Kami can do their thing. I’ll do mine.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “For nutjobs who set their own stuff on fire to paint pictures…”
MENU: “They’re pretty good at covering their tracks.”
MINA: “Not many clues, but plenty of blue pansies.”
MINA: “Plenty of magic, too.”

objectives.description: Make 3 Scavenging Rolls at Flower Mural

title: Sigil Magic
introDialogue:
MINA: “I believe that flower is more than just a pretty drawing.”
MINA: “Have you heard of sigil magic?”
MINA: “It’s quite old, but had a modern revival some decades past.”
MINA: “You may have read about it in a comic book.”
MINA: “To seal your magic in a single image. That’s what this is.”
MINA: “Let your Kami out to harvest and search more thoroughly.”
MINA: “I wonder about those scraps.”
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “What was the book sacrificed to draw this?”
MINA: “You might think it doesn’t matter. You’d be a fool.”
MINA: “It might be everything.”

objectives.description: Harvest for >360 minutes at Flower Mural

title: Sigil Magic II
introDialogue:
MINA: “Give me some flowers and plastics.”
MINA: “I want to make some charms and cast some divinations on my own.”
MINA: “You’ve been a good servant. Deliver the reagents immediately and uphold that reputation.”
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “Maybe a lure? No, perhaps a repellent. Is it possible that it’s apotropaic?”
MINA: “Oh, to hell with this.”
MINA: “I feel like I need to step down there personally to check this out.”
MINA: “And I am absolutely not doing that.”
MENU: “The probability of it being a trap does appear high.”

title: The Crystal Set
introDialogue:
MENU: “I feel a little sick in here.”
MENU: “Uh.”
MENU: “Mina, you take this one. My vision is bugging out.”
MINA: “Operator, take stock of the materials here.”
MINA: “And make it quick.”
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “I see why our friend was uncomfortable.”
MINA: “I feel it too.” 
MINA: “This crystal is radiating with incredible magical power.”
MINA: “It feels just like the leftovers in the broken tube.”
MINA: “A setting like this appears more natural than that prison…”
MINA: “But still, something feels wrong.”

objectives.description: Make 3 Scavenging Rolls at Radiant Crystal

title: The Crystal Set II
introDialogue:
MENU: “Get out of there for a minute.”
MENU: “I can think better in the other rooms, at least.”
MENU: “Head back to the Reinforced Tunnel where that book is.”
MENU: “I’ll do some cross referencing. Maybe I’ll understand it better.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “So the crystal is like, the channeling point for some kind of zero point vacuum energy?”
MENU: “And it’s kept in check by a polygonal unified field?”
MINA: “I don’t follow you at all.”
MENU: “I barely trust what I’m saying myself.”

title: The Crystal Set III
introDialogue:
MENU: “I think I’m getting used to that room the more you visit.”
MENU: “There’s a unique item to be found by the Radiant Crystal. Did you know?”
MENU: “The roots wrapped around the crystal absorb the energy it radiates.”
MENU: “Some of them fall off, but they keep that blue radiance.” 
MENU: “Grab a few.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “This seems like the sort of item that only ever shows up in one place.”
MENU: “Something unpleasant about holding these things, too.”
MENU: “Kind of like the crystal itself, but weaker.”
MENU: “I’ll get used to it.”

title: The Crystal Set IV
introDialogue:
MINA: “I think that I’ve properly established how distasteful I find the act of drawing power from this crystal.”
MINA: “With that said, I’ve also been considering the ethics of using these roots in hexerei.”
MINA: “If the kami just happen to pick up whats already there…”
MINA: “It’s acceptable.”
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “Hmm… Indeed, something unpleasant about holding this.”
MINA: “Though I can feel the potential.” 
MINA: “Get your tools ready, apprentice. Let’s try something new.”

title: The Crystal Set V
introDialogue:
MINA: “Make an extract of the root, but leave one unprocessed for later.”
MINA: “I’ve got two recipes in mind.”
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “I’m still not entirely comfortable working with this reagent.”
MINA: “Ah well. There’s nothing to be done.”

title: Beyond the Pale
introDialogue:
MINA: “Mix the extract with a little mint and steep with freshly boiled water.”
MINA: “Let it rest, then bottle it. Shake before drinking.”
MINA: “Go on, give it a try.”
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “These roots produce a startling effect on your Kami.”
MINA: “But a very useful one.”
MINA: “I’ll look the other way if you decide to use more of these potions.”

title: Tracing Your Roots
introDialogue:
MENU: “So where do those roots go?”
MENU: “Like up on the surface. A tree, I guess. But what tree?”
MINA: “If the Operator is smart, they may have figured it out already.”
MINA: “But if not, we can help them along.”
MENU: “You already know?”
MINA: “Operator. Take this list of ingredients. I’ve got something I want you to build.”
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “I’ve been quite vocal about it… The crystal’s power, I mean.”
MINA: “Sapping it for one’s own uses is distasteful, yes.”
MINA: “And I don’t celebrate that sort of thing.”
MINA: “But we must sometimes do distasteful things to win.”

objectives.description: Give 1 Ingot,Give 1 Essence of Sight,Give 1 Irradiated Root

rewards.description: 2x Agency Reputation,1x Ingot,1x Irradiated Root,1x Essence of Sight

title: Rhabdomancy
introDialogue:
MINA: “I’ve given you plans for a sort of dowsing rod.”
MINA: “The ingot to craft a housing, the root for a handle, and an essence of sight for the sensor.”
MINA: “Menu’s technical language may be rubbing off.”
MINA: “Craft the device. We’ll see what it turns up.”
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “The rod isn’t a guiding hand to point you through the dark.”
MINA: “It will respond when you get close to the right places.”
MINA: “You should be able to use it to find the crystal’s tree above ground.”

title: Bitter Fruits
introDialogue:
MINA: “The rod senses something.” 
MINA: “You see what I meant? You might have figured this out already, Operator.”
MINA: “Not many trees that look like this.”
MINA: “Get a little closer and buy something. I want to see how it reacts.”
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “Awful.”
MINA: “It gave me the shivers, even from the distance of scrying.”
MINA: “There’s more than just the crystal in the caves.”
MINA: “Something else is living in that tree. Tied in to those roots.”

requirements.description: Complete MSQ094,Own the Dowsing Rod,In Room: Marketplace

objectives.description: Use the machine to buy something in the Marketplace

title: Into the Canyon
introDialogue:
MENU: “That thing almost looks real.”
MINA: “It does a real enough job of holding our Operator back.”
MINA: “And the magic is so thick in the air you can taste it.”
MENU: “I knew you were sharing sight, but taste?”
MINA: “It’s just an expression.”
MINA: “But spend some time in here and taste for yourself.”
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “Talismans all over the walls.”
MINA: “These magicians had a powerful desire to seal the darkness below”
MINA: “Humans put too much faith in paper.”

objectives.description: Harvest for >360 minutes at Giant's Palm

title: Back of my Hand
introDialogue:
MINA: “The closer you get to opening this seal, the stronger the sense of foreboding that I feel.”
MINA: “It’s almost as if the caves drain my spirit through my connection to you.”
MINA: “Do me a favor, servant.”
MINA: “Give me an energy drink?”
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “You are appreciated.”
MINA: “As a servant, you rank among the top five thousand.”
MINA: “At the lowest.”

title: Heartwood
introDialogue:
MENU: “That dowsing rod is wiggling again.”
MINA: “The Blooming Tree has a certain presence. I’m not surprised.”
MINA: “Something similar may have happened here.”
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “It’s possible this tree once held a great crystal heart.”
MINA: “Something of similar size and power as the great crystal in the caves.”
MENU: “And someone stole it away?”
MENU: “If we don’t find it in down in those tunnels, I wonder where it went…”

requirements.description: Complete MSQ094,Own the Dowsing Rod,In Room: Blooming Tree

objectives.description: Make 1 Scavenging Roll at Blooming Tree

title: Hypersensitivity
introDialogue:
MENU: “The dowsing rod is going off here? Is it bugged?”
MENU: “Mina, what’s the deal?”
MINA: “My shop is small. It’s a tight space. The dowsing rod is probably just picking up the leftover residue on the operator.”
MINA: “Check outside my door, in the open air. You won’t find anything, I’m sure.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “…Yeah, nothing out here.”
MENU: “I guess she was right.”
MENU: “But I don’t think that really proved anything.”

requirements.description: Complete MSQ094,Own the Dowsing Rod,In Room: Convenience Store

title: Prima Facie
introDialogue:
MENU: “What kind of creepy decoration is this?”
MINA: “Looks like a skeleton.”
MENU: “Yeah, but it must be a model. The craftsmanship is really impressive.”
MENU: “Operator, check this place out for a while with your Kami. I can run an analysis.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “I must be bugging out again.”
MENU: “It’s solid bone, but that doesn’t make much sense.”
MINA: “My friend, sometimes things are just how they appear.”
MENU: “Yeah, well, if someone’s trying to creep me out by spoofing my sensors, it’s working.”

objectives.description: Harvest for >360 minutes at Guardian Skull

title: Pillars of the Earth
introDialogue:
MINA: “Our dear friend Menu is having a hard time accepting the reality of this great corpse.”
MINA: “Yes, of course it’s real. In fact, I recognize him.”
MINA: “He was an old friend and mentor. Someone I thought would hang on forever.”
MINA: “I’d like you to pay respects on my behalf.”
MINA: “He loved a drink. Pour out something strong for him.”
MINA: “This may not be a proper grave, but he would appreciate the gesture all the same.”
resolutionDialogue:
MINA: “Thank you. You’ve been truly generous.”
MINA: “I’ve had a great deal of fun condescending to you and treating you as my servant, but I want to make something clear.”
MINA: “You’re more than that. Absolutely not an equal, of course, but I’ve grown to respect you. I may have even begun to enjoy your company.”
MINA: “Now then. That’s all the sentimentality you’ll get from me for some time.”
MINA: “Goodbye.”

objectives.description: Move to Guardian Skull,Give 1 Toadstool Liquor

title: Axis Mundi
introDialogue:
MENU: “This is the deepest part of the cave system.”
MENU: “We’re deep below the central point of Kamigotchi World’s axial mountain.”
MENU: “The location just feels important.”
MENU: “These pillars and brickwork don’t match anything else in the caves, aesthetically. Let me run some scans.
MENU: “You might trust the results more than I do, though.” 
MENU: “I’ll check the marble. You grab some stuff from the room to compare.”
resolutionDialogue:
MENU: “Weirdest thing.”
MENU: “These old marble pillars? Every one I check is just plaster with distressing. They just look old.”
MENU: “This whole temple might have been built within the last fifty years.”
